sourcePrompt: An M14 battle rifle, early-war US issue, about one point one meters long and traditional in profile. A full-length rifle with a one-piece walnut stock and handguard running most of its length, a long barrel ending in a slotted flash suppressor, a twenty-round box magazine below the receiver, and iron sights front and rear. Walnut wood and parkerized steel, upright and robust.
